revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[仁賢天皇]]2年（[[489年]]）以前-没年不詳）は[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事...'
revision.text:
'''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[仁賢天皇]]2年（[[489年]]）以前-没年不詳）は[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は仁賢天皇、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]の皇后、[[敏達天皇]]の母）の母。
宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[535年]][[4月25日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后される。
== 系譜 == *父：仁賢天皇 *母：春日大娘皇女 *同母兄弟：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 *夫：宣化天皇 **子：石姫皇女・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明）
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たちばなのなかつひめみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の皇族]]

revision.comment: 合葬の記事を追加
revision.text:
'''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[仁賢天皇]]2年（[[489年]]）以前-没年不詳）は[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は仁賢天皇、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]の皇后、[[敏達天皇]]の母）の母。
宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[535年]][[4月25日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后された。[[崩御]]後、宣化天皇[[陵]]に合葬された。
== 系譜 == *父：仁賢天皇 *母：春日大娘皇女 *同母兄弟：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 *夫：宣化天皇 **子：石姫皇女・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明）

== 系譜 == * 父：仁賢天皇 * 母：春日大娘皇女 * 同母兄弟：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 * 夫：宣化天皇 ** 子：石姫皇女・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明）
{{歴代皇后一覧}}

（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）}}]] '''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は[[仁賢天皇]]、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）の母。 宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[535年]][[4月25日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后された。[[崩御]]後、孺子（幼い子）と共に宣化天皇[[陵]]に合葬された。天皇と合葬された皇后は、他には[[天武天皇]]の皇后である[[持統天皇]]だけである。 == 系譜 == * 父：[[仁賢天皇]] * 母：[[春日大娘皇女]] * 同母兄弟姉妹：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 * 夫：宣化天皇 ** 子：[[石姫皇女]]・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たちはなのなかつひめみこ}} [[Category:6世紀日本の女性]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:古墳時代の后妃]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）}}]] '''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は[[仁賢天皇]]、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）の母。 宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[535年]][[4月25日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后された。[[崩御]]後、孺子（幼い子）と共に宣化天皇[[陵]]に合葬された。天皇と合葬された皇后は、他には[[安閑天皇]]の皇后である[[春日山田皇女]]（『[[日本書紀]]』による。現在の治定は別）と[[天武天皇]]の皇后である[[持統天皇]]だけである。 == 系譜 == * 父：[[仁賢天皇]] * 母：[[春日大娘皇女]] * 同母兄弟姉妹：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 * 夫：宣化天皇 ** 子：[[石姫皇女]]・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たちはなのなかつひめみこ}} [[Category:6世紀日本の女性]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代の后妃]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:仁賢天皇の子女]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）}}]] '''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は[[仁賢天皇]]、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）の母。 宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[536年]][[4月14日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后された。[[崩御]]後、孺子（幼い子）と共に宣化天皇[[陵]]に合葬された。天皇と合葬された皇后は、他には[[安閑天皇]]の皇后である[[春日山田皇女]]（『[[日本書紀]]』による。現在の治定は別）と[[天武天皇]]の皇后である[[持統天皇]]だけである。 == 系譜 == * 父：[[仁賢天皇]] * 母：[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]皇女） * 同母兄弟姉妹：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]后）・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 * 夫：宣化天皇 ** 子：[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たちはなのなかつひめみこ}} [[Category:6世紀日本の女性皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代の后妃]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:仁賢天皇の子女]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}) * Siblings (same parents): - Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi - Asatsuma no Ōkimi - {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}}) - Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi - {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇}} - Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} - Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳}}

She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=draft}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{citation needed|Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{citation needed|Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} {{draft categories| [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}}

She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{citation needed|Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{citation needed|Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Empresses of Japan]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Consorts of the Kofun period]] [[Category:Women before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Unknown birth and death dates]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[Category:Expanded pages from ]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}}

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=draft|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=draft|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|12=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. 倉稚綾姫皇女
日影皇女
上殖葉皇子 |養子女 = |女御宣下 = |皇太夫人 = |准后 = |身位 = |皇居 = |宮廷首脳人物 = |宮廷首脳人物2 = |宮廷女房 = |地位 = |立后前位階 = |栄典 = |役職 = |親署 = |脚注 = }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[宣化天皇]]・橘仲皇女
[[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|身狹桃花鳥坂上陵]]
（[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]）}}]] '''橘仲皇女'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[宣化天皇]]の[[皇后]]。[[古事記]]には橘之中比売命（たちばなのなかつひめのみこと）とある。父は[[仁賢天皇]]、母は[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]の[[皇女]]）。[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）の母。 宣化天皇元年[[3月8日 (旧暦)|3月8日]]（[[536年]][[4月14日]]）に宣化天皇の皇后に立后された。[[崩御]]後、孺子（幼い子）と共に宣化天皇[[陵]]に合葬された。天皇と合葬された皇后は、他には[[安閑天皇]]の皇后である[[春日山田皇女]]（『[[日本書紀]]』による。現在の治定は別）と[[天武天皇]]の皇后である[[持統天皇]]だけである。 == 系譜 == * 父：[[仁賢天皇]] * 母：[[春日大娘皇女]]（[[雄略天皇]]皇女） * 同母兄弟姉妹：高橋大娘皇女・朝嬬皇女・[[手白香皇女]]（[[継体天皇]]后）・樟氷皇女・[[武烈天皇]]・真稚皇女 * 夫：宣化天皇 ** 子：[[石姫皇女]]（[[欽明天皇]]后）・小石姫皇女・倉稚綾姫皇女・日影皇女・上殖葉皇子・某（夭逝、男女不明） {{歴代皇后一覧}} {{歴代日本の皇太后一覧}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たちはなのなかつひめみこ}} [[Category:6世紀日本の女性皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇后]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:古墳時代の后妃]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:仁賢天皇の子女]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
