{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} ** Children: - {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}) - Princess Koishi - Princess Kurawata Ayahime - Princess Hikage - Prince Ueshikiba - A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|橘仲皇女|version=98176427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:]] }} [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[sn:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]]

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|ja|武烈天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ninken|qq=}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|qq=}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ishi|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kasuga no Yamada|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ankan|qq=}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kasuga no Yamada|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ankan|qq=}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ninken|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|qq=}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ninken|qq=}}, and her mother was {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|qq=}}, the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi|ja|石姫皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ishi|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|ja|春日山田皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kasuga no Yamada|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ankan|qq=}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|ja|仁賢天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ninken|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|ja|春日大娘皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|qq=}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Te-shiraga|ja|手白香皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Te-shiraga|qq=}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=}}. {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|橘仲皇女|version=98176427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese people]] [[Category:Daughters of Japanese emperors]] [[sn:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] [[Category:6世紀日本の女性皇族]] [[Category:仁賢天皇の子女]] [[Category:古墳時代の后妃]] [[Category:古墳時代以前の女性皇族]] [[Category:日本の皇太后]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]] [[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]] [[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]] [[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]] [[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]

Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the {{ill|Princess|ja|皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess|qq=}} of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|qid=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|qid=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|qid=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|qid=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|qid=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|qid=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|qid=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|qid=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|qid=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|qid=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|qid=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|qid=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|qid=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|qid=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|qid=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|qid=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|qid=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|qid=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|qid=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|qid=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|qid=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|qid=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|qid=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|qid=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|qid=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|qid=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|qid=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|qid=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|qid=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|qid=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|qid=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|橘仲皇女|version=98176427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[ja:橘仲皇女]] [[Category:Japanese Female Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Ninken]] [[Category:Imperial Consorts of the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Empress Dowagers of Japan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]] [[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]] [[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]] [[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]] [[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11121925}}
