[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|qq=|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}

[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, gender unknown) ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|橘仲皇女|version=98176427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] ناکاتسو]] [[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]] [[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11121925}}

[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{People-substub}} ==Issue== **{{Nihongo|[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]]|石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Oishi-hime]]|小石姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=536–539}} {{succession box | before= [[Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] | title=[[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] | years=appointed in 539}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|橘仲皇女|version=98176427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese women]] The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11121925]] Wikidata name: Tachibana no Nakatsu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] |issue = {{plainlist| *[[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi-hime]] *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] and [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]]. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], and her mother was [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]], the [[:en:Princess|Princess]] of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]. She was the mother of [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Yamada|Princess Kasuga no Yamada]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]], according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], although the current official designation is different) and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi]] (Princess of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Te-shiraga]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] ** Children: *** [[:en:Princess Ishi-hime|Princess Ishi]] (Empress of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi
{{ill|Toriya Misanzai Kofun|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriya Misanzai Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]])}}]] == See Also == * {{ill|List of Empresses of Japan|ja|歴代皇后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empresses of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja|歴代日本の皇太后一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Empress Dowagers of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Imperial-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|[[Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime]]|倉稚綾姫皇女}}, married to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] **{{Nihongo|Prince Kamiewa|上殖葉皇子}} **Child (died early, – fl. 539) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} and {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} and {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} |issue = {{plainlist| *{{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |father = {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} and {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

revision.text:
[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749}}}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} |issue = {{plainlist| *{{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime}} *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} |father = {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} and {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=8th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|lt=Princess Te-shiraga}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}) ** Kusu no Hihara no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu}} ** Mawataka no Ōkimi * Husband: {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} ** Children: *** {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}) *** Princess Koishi *** Princess Kurawata Ayahime *** Princess Hikage *** Prince Ueshikiba *** A child (unknown gender, died young) [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi

[[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] [[en:Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu]][[fa:شاهدخت تاچیبانا نو ناکاتسو]][[ja:橘仲皇女]][[ko:다치바나노나카쓰히메미코]][[zh:橘仲皇女]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}|children={{ill|Hikage-kōjo|WD=Q106096224|1=Hikage-kōjo}}; {{ill|Ishihime|WD=Q11585498|1=Princess Ishi-hime}}; {{ill|Kura no Wayaka-Hime|WD=Q106097469|1=Kura no Wayaka-Hime}}; {{ill|Oishi-hime no ojō|WD=Q111303300|1=Oishi-hime no ojō}}; {{ill|Q111304973|WD=Q111304973|1=Kamushiba no Miko}}|father={{ill|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|1=Emperor Ninken}}|mother={{ill|Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Kōgō|WD=Q11088800|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}|relatives={{ill|Buretsu|WD=Q334644|1=Emperor Buretsu}}; {{ill|Kasuga no Yamada no Himemiko|WD=Q11088803|1=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}}; {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|WD=Q11075749|1=Princess Tashiraka}}}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|橘仲皇女|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Tachibana no Nakatsu |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} |reign = 536–539 |succession1 = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = appointed in 539 |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} |issue = {{plainlist| *{{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} *Princess Oishi-hime *Princess Kura-no-wakaya-hime *Prince Kamiewa *Unknown child}} |royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} |father = {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}} '''Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu''' (? – fl. 539) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}. Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co. She gave birth to five children. Tachibana no Nakatsu was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} and {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}. She became empress dowager from 539. '''Tachibana no Naka no Ōkimi''' (橘仲皇女, birth and death dates unknown) was the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|WD=Q7723211}} of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, she is referred to as Tachibana no Naka no Hime no Mikoto (橘之中比売命). Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess|1=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=March 8 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=Q28686274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada|1=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}} (Princess of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}) * Siblings (same parents): ** Takahashi no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi ** Asatsuma no Ōkimi ** {{ill|Princess Tashiraka|en|Princess Her father was {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}, and her mother was {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|en|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|WD=Q11088800|lt=Kasuga no Ōiratsume no Ōkimi|1=Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume}}, the {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess|1=Princess}} of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. She was the mother of {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|WD=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}). She was enthroned as the Empress of Emperor Senka on {{ill|March 8 (old calendar)|ja|3月8日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 8 (old calendar)|1=March 8 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q838873}} in the first year of his reign ({{ill|April 14, 536|ja|536年4月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 14, 536|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). After her death, she was interred alongside Emperor Senka and their young children in the same {{ill|tomb|ja|陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁治定陵墓の一覧|1=Q28686274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686274}}. The only other Empresses known to have been interred with their Emperors are {{ill|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|en|Princess Kasuga no Yamada|WD=Q11088803|lt=Princess Kasuga no Yamada|1=Princess Kasuga no Yamada}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|en|Emperor Ankan|WD=Q241765|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor Ankan}}, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, although the current official designation is different) and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} (Empress of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). == Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}} * Mother: {{ill|Princess
